body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"acceptance criteria dropped or altered","fingerprint":"9a9f3e7563c088c6559b50170a909615619d5996d3af04d242043c3656f19c85","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"rewrite_rejected","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.rewrite_rejected","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"rationale":"The bead has explicit parent/deps, a named `TestADRMutationConformance`, the required `go test` and `lefthook` gates, and enough implementation intent for a resolver-lint task. It still misses the rubric on title form, structured description with file:line-grounded current state/root cause and non-scope, and an `area:*` label; it also relies on `/tmp/story-15-final.md` instead of embedding durable context.","score":5,"suggested_fixes":["Rewrite the title as an imperative subsystem-scoped change, e.g. `graphql: add ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers`.","Reformat the description into PROBLEM / ROOT CAUSE or CURRENT STATE / PROPOSED FIX / NON-SCOPE, cite the relevant `cli/internal/server/graphql` file:line(s), and inline the needed test/risk excerpt instead of referencing `/tmp/story-15-final.md`.","Add an `area:graphql` (or equivalent server/graphql area) label so the bead includes phase, area, kind, and cross-ref facets.","Enumerate the explicit allowlist exceptions in the bead body, with the specific resolver names or files they apply to."],"waivers_applied":[]}
